title: Neuroergonomic 2021 dataset
description: A multi-session EEG dataset acquired from 15 healthy participants performing resting state and graded cognitive tasks (MATB-II at three difficulty levels). The dataset comprises 62-channel EEG recordings sampled at 500 Hz, designed for passive brain-computer interface applications and mental workload estimation in neuroergonomic contexts. This dataset includes raw EEG data with HED event annotations and is intended for cross-subject and cross-session machine learning studies.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using an ActiCHamp system (Brain Products GmbH) with 62 active Ag/AgCl electrodes positioned according to the standard 10-20 montage, referenced to Fpz. Sampling rate was 500 Hz with a 50 Hz line frequency. Impedance was maintained below 25 kOhm. Participants completed two sessions each, performing resting state and three difficulty levels of the MATB-II task. An auxiliary ECG channel was recorded.

funding_references.funder_name: ANITI (Artificial and Natural Intelligence Toulouse Institute)

funding_references.award_title: Artificial and Natural Intelligence Toulouse Institute
